unique_vulnerabilities.name: Reentrancy in withdrawFunds()
unique_vulnerabilities.reasoning: These findings describe a reentrancy vulnerability involving an external call on line 27 and subsequent state changes on lines 28-29. Both tools (Slither and Mythril) agree on the issue.

unique_vulnerabilities.name: Dependence on block.timestamp in withdrawFunds()
unique_vulnerabilities.reasoning: Both findings highlight the use of block.timestamp for control flow decisions in the withdrawFunds() function. This is a time manipulation vulnerability.

unique_vulnerabilities.name: Outdated Solidity version
unique_vulnerabilities.reasoning: Both findings point to the use of an outdated Solidity version (0.4.x), which has known vulnerabilities. These are informational warnings.

unique_vulnerabilities.name: Non-constant state variable
unique_vulnerabilities.reasoning: This finding suggests that the withdrawalLimit variable should be marked as constant for gas optimization. It is unrelated to other issues.

unique_vulnerabilities.name: Improper naming convention
unique_vulnerabilities.reasoning: This finding highlights a naming convention issue for the _weiToWithdraw parameter. It is purely stylistic and unrelated to other vulnerabilities.
